revision.comment: Reverted 2 edits by [[Special:Contributions/223.178.83.195|223.178.83.195]] ([[User talk:223.178.83.195|talk]]): Unexplained deletions
revision.text:
{{Wikipedia how to|WP:CSVAN}} {{notice|This guide needs improvement and expansion.}} Citation Style Vancouver (CSVAN) is a method of referencing Wikipedia articles using a series of templates based on the [[Vancouver system]]. The use of CSVAN or of templates is not compulsory; per [[WP:CITEVAR]]:
Citations within each Wikipedia article should follow a consistent style. Editors may choose any style they want. The English Wikipedia does not have a house style, so it need not match what is done in other articles.
==Templates== CSVAN consists of the following set of templates: * {{tl|vcite book}} for books * {{tl|vcite conference}} for conference proceedings * {{tl|vcite journal}} for articles in academic journals and similar periodicals * {{tl|vcite news}} for articles in newspapers and similar news sources * {{tl|vcite web}} for web pages, that is, sources published as part of web sites that are not books, journals, or news {{Wikipedia referencing}} [[Category:Citation Style Vancouver templates|*]]

==Templates== CSVAN consists of the following set of templates: * {{tl|vcite book}} for books * {{tl|vcite conference}} for conference proceedings * {{tl|vcite journal}} for articles in academic journals and similar periodicals * {{tl|vcite news}} for articles in newspapers and similar news sources * {{tl|vcite web}} for web pages, that is, sources published as part of web sites that are not books, journals, or news {{Wikipedia referencing}} [[Category:Citation Style Vancouver templates|*]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] [[en:Citation Style Vancouver]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[en:Citation Style Vancouver]]

==Templates== CSVAN consists of the following set of templates: * {{tl|vcite book}} for books * {{tl|vcite conference}} for conference proceedings * {{tl|vcite journal}} for articles in academic journals and similar periodicals * {{tl|vcite news}} for articles in newspapers and similar news sources * {{tl|vcite web}} for web pages, that is, sources published as part of web sites that are not books, journals, or news {{Wikipedia referencing}} [[Category:Citation Style Vancouver templates|*]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 4]][[Category:11 4]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 4]][[Category:11 4]] [[en:Citation Style Vancouver]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 4]][[Category:11 4]] [[en:Citation Style Vancouver]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Citation Style Vancouver]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Citation Style Vancouver]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Citation Style Vancouver}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]]
